'''Iosia Soliola''' (born 4 August 1986) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL). He played for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international level.

He previously played as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the NRL, and as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], with whom he won the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]]. Whilst playing in Europe he gained selection for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Soliola was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand. He is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.eventpolynesia.com/news&info/nz/galleryarchive/NZ2_page_gal26nov06.htm|title=Home Based Writing Jobs and How They Are Helping Writers}}</ref>

After making his début in Jersey Flegg, Soliola moved onto [[NSW Cup|Premier League]] in which he played with Eli Hayes and won a premiership with the Roosters in 2004.

==Professional playing career==
[[File:Iosia Soliola.JPG|thumb|left|Soliola playing for New Zealand in 2009]]
[[File:Iosia.jpg|thumb|left|Iosia Soliola with the Sydney Roosters]]

===2000s===
In 2005, Soliola made his NRL début for the Roosters against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]], and went on to play 19 games for the club that season.

He was chosen to make his international début at the age of 19 for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in the [[Rugby League Tri-Nations|Tri-nations series]] of 2005, however was forced to pull out due to injury.

Soliola then went on to represent New Zealand in the [[2006 Rugby League Tri-Nations|2006 Tri-nations tournament]]. In 5 games, Soliola scored 4 tries including a double against the [[Australian national rugby league team|Australians]] at the [[Telstra Dome]] in [[Melbourne]].

Soliola was selected to play for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand national team]] at centre in the 2007 [[ANZAC Test]] loss against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Anzac Test Match 2007|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/anzac-test-match-2007/australia-vs-new-zealand.html|work=rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=Shawn Dollin, Andrew Ferguson and Bill Bates|access-date=1 September 2013}}</ref> He was dropped to premier league in the middle of the 2007 season before returning to first grade in round 14 and regained his form before he badly injured his knee in round 22 against the [[Wests Tigers]]. He missed the rest of the 2007 season.

Soliola returned from his lengthy spell on the sidelines in the off season to come off the bench in the Roosters' opening centenary clash against South Sydney in round 1. He also represented the Kiwis in the 100 year centenary test match where he scored one try.

In August, 2008 Soliola was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref name="Kiwis select Sonny Bill">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949034,00.html|title=Kiwis select Sonny Bill|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-08|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref> and in September, 2008  he was selected in the final 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#New Zealand|New Zealand]].<ref name="Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7656238.stm|title=Kiwis to wait on Webb and Matai|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He was forced to withdraw from the squad due to a shoulder injury.<ref name="New Zealand hit by Soliola injury">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7681663.stm|title=New Zealand hit by Soliola injury|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-21|access-date=2008-10-22}}</ref>

The Sydney Roosters confirmed that Soliola had signed a three-year deal to play with English [[Super League]] club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] midway through the [[2009 NRL Season]]. Soliola played his last match for the tri colours against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], ending his 6-year stint with the club. Soliola also represented the Kiwis in the [[2009 Four Nations]] tournament.

===2010s===
Soliola officially made his début for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in 2010 making the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]].

Iosia Soliola was selected for the [[Exiles rugby league team|Exiles]] squad for the inaugural [[Rugby League International Origin Match]] against [[England national rugby league team|England]].

In the 2011 game Soliola was supposed to play in the 2nd Row alongside [[Willie Manu]], but due to the birth of [[Warrington Wolves]]' [[Matt King (rugby league)|Matt King]]'s baby, Soliola was moved by coach [[Brian McLennan]] to the centre.

New Exiles coach [[Daniel Anderson (rugby league)|Daniel Anderson]] selected Soliola at centre for Game 1 of the International Origin. However he was moved into the loose forward role for Game 2 in which the Exiles won 32-20.

He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=21 November 2019}}</ref>

He captained [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

On 12 April 2014, Iosia announced he would end his [[Super League]] career at the conclusion of the 2014 season.,<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29970/soliola-to-end-super-league |title=Soliola to end Super League stint |publisher=Superleague |date=12 April 2014 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140413124026/http://www.superleague.co.uk/article/29970/soliola-to-end-super-league |archive-date=13 April 2014}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www1.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9261450/super-league-sia-soliola-to-leave-st-helens-and-return-to-the-nrl-at-end-of-2014|title=Super League: Sia Soliola to leave St Helens and return to the NRL at end of 2014}}</ref> He agreed join the [[Canberra Raiders]] in 2015 on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10347364/Iosia-Soliola-joins-the-Raiders-from-St-Helens|title = Iosia Soliola joins the Raiders from St Helens|date = 6 August 2014}}</ref>

St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and Soliola was selected to play at second-row forward, scoring a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their 14-6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|access-date=12 October 2014|work= [[BBC Sport]]|publisher= [[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref>

===2015===
Soliola came back to Australia to play with the [[Canberra Raiders]].  In his first season back in the [[NRL]], Soliola made 19 appearances and scored 4 tries.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

===2016===
In the [[2016 NRL season]], Soliola was part of the Canberra side which made it through to their first preliminary final in nearly 20 years.  Canberra went on to lose the match against Melbourne 14-12.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/nrl-finals-2016-melbourne-storm-v-canberra-raiders-preliminary-final-player-ratings/news-story/fc1f07f729febfe98d5ead06ad1e2b2a|title = NRL finals 2016: Melbourne Storm v Canberra Raiders preliminary final player ratings|date = 24 September 2016}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-09-24/melbourne-storm-reach-nrl-grand-final-with-win-over-canberra/7874856|title = Storm to meet Cronulla in NRL grand final after 14-12 win over Raiders|newspaper = ABC News|date = 24 September 2016}}</ref>

===2017===
In the [[2017 NRL season]], Soliola was suspended for 5 weeks after a high shot on Melbourne's [[Billy Slater]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/soliola-suspended-for-five-weeks/tabid/10874/newsid/110371/default.aspx|title=Soliola suspended for five weeks|date=25 July 2017}}</ref>  On 2 September 2017, Soliola made his 150th appearance in the [[NRL]] for Canberra in their match against the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://commentaryboxsports.com/australian/nrl/stats-amazing-nrl-round-26-2.html |title=STATS AMAZING! NRL ROUND 26 |access-date=4 September 2017 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170905054209/http://commentaryboxsports.com/australian/nrl/stats-amazing-nrl-round-26-2.html |archive-date=5 September 2017 |url-status=dead }}</ref>

===2018===
On 3 July, Soliola signed a two-year contract extension to keep him at Canberra until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/soliola-re-signs-with-raiders/|title=Soliola re-signs with Canberra|website=SBS}}</ref>

===2019===
Soliola made 26 appearances for Canberra in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club reached the grand final for the first time in 25 years.  Soliola played at prop in the club's [[2019 NRL Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Sydney Roosters]] at [[ANZ Stadium]].  Soliola was awarded the [[Ken Stephen Medal]] at the official awards ceremony following the game for his involvement with the community in relation to [[rugby league]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings-roosters-and-raiders-hits-and-misses-20191006-p52y53.html/|title=NRL grand final player ratings: Roosters and Raiders hits and misses|website=SMH|date=6 October 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49950757|title=Sydney Roosters beat Canberra Raiders to win NRL Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/grand-final-ricky-stuart-reaction-canberra-raiders-six-again-jack-wighton/ae39b639-d375-4608-89a6-af6285c3d635/|title=How Ricky Stuart reacted to grand final 'six-again' refereeing shocker|website=WWOS|date=6 October 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
After playing the first eight games of the [[2020 NRL season]] for the Raiders, Soliola suffered a horrific facial fracture during their 22-16 win against [[St. George Illawarra]] on 3 July. He returned from the injury for Canberra's round 20 match against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and played a part in the side's finals campaign. On 6 October it was announced Soliola had re-signed with Canberra for the 2021 NRL season.<ref>{{cite web |title=Soliola re-signs with Raiders ahead of Roosters rematch |url=https://canberraweekly.com.au/sia-soliola-re-signs-with-raiders-ahead-of-roosters-rematch/ |website=Canberra Weekly |date=8 October 2020 |publisher=Newstate Media}}</ref>

He played in all three of Canberra's finals games including the preliminary final defeat against Melbourne.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/ricky-stuart-storms-out-of-canberra-raiders-press-conference-after-one-question/edab3d4d-0eeb-4960-acc0-89d06797f8e0/|title=Canberra Raiders coach Ricky Stuart storms out of press conference after one question|website=wwos.nine.com.au|date=16 October 2020 }}</ref>

===2021===
On 28 September, Soliola announced his retirement from rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/09/28/fan-favourite-soliola-hangs-up-the-boots/|title=Fan favourite Soliola calls time on 17-year career|website=www.nrl.com|date=28 September 2021}}</ref>
